experiments.hypothesis: Rewriting the App Store description with approved Garmin sentence and tighter opening will produce a clear, credible listing that converts at ≥35% (product page → install) after first 100 impressions
Instrumenting onboarding_completed, plan_generated, and run_logged before launch will make the first 30 days of installs measurable, enabling data-driven iteration from week 2
Moving users from plan_generated to first run with a concrete first-run commitment and local reminder will improve first-run activation before D7 can be evaluated
The six WP-43 Tier A entry-funnel changes will increase the share of qualified landers who submit the free checker
Post-launch ASO cleanup, first-review prompt, subtitle/keyword review, and screenshot captions will improve App Store product page → install conversion once baseline impressions exist
Adding approved A-variant caption overlays to all 5 screenshot slots will improve App Store conversion rate vs bare UI screenshots, measured at next keyword report
Sending welcome + plan-nudge + 2-day no-show emails via Resend/Supabase will increase week-1 activation rate (plan generated → first run logged) vs no email baseline

Writing the Resumely App Store listing (subtitle Option A, keywords field v1, description v1) will produce a conversion-optimised listing ready for first submission and establish a baseline for subsequent A/B iteration
experiments.status: approved — ready to file in ASC

experiments.hypothesis: Producing a 5-slot screenshot brief with keyword-rich captions (indexed since 2025) and structured copy overlays will give the listing strong visual conversion signals from day one
experiments.status: approved — screenshots exported; PR #34 ready to merge; upload to ASC pending

experiments.hypothesis: Submitting Resumely to 5 AI/career directories (Futurepedia, TAAFT, Toolify, AI Tool Hunt, Launching Next) will produce initial backlinks and referral traffic to the web funnel that feeds App Store installs

Publishing a Hebrew subtitle, keyword field, promo text, and 5 screenshot captions (Fit-First framing, per WP-31) will improve Israeli storefront App Store product page → install conversion within a 21-day measurement window
experiments.status: queued — WP-31 ready, founder-approved, asset pack not yet drafted

models.source: July 2026 capability review (founder + ChatGPT draft). Pricing independently verified 2026-07-10: Claude via claude-api skill; GPT-5.6 / Grok 4.5 / Composer 2.5 via web search. All figures confirmed against the draft.
models.note: Model selection is a RECOMMENDATION, not a rule. The `harness` column shows where each model runs natively, but routes are advisory — you can run any task in whichever tool you choose (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, xAI) and pick a different model than the one suggested. Canonical policy: GLOBAL-TOOL-USAGE.md 'Model routing'.

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.detail: Native subagents (model: frontmatter), /model per session, workflows (opts.model per stage).

harnesses.name: Codex
harnesses.detail: GPT-5.6 family. Multi-step repo tasks, terminal/CI, cross-repo, prod verification, cross-vendor review of Claude work.

harnesses.name: Cursor Composer
harnesses.detail: Composer 2.5. Localized edits, boilerplate, mechanical refactors, UI iteration from an explicit plan.

harnesses.name: Grok (xAI)
harnesses.detail: Grok 4.5. Live web/X research, terminal-heavy work, parallel cheap exploration. Only if wired into the active harness.

models.role: Exceptional escalation — architecture authority, unresolved failures, final reasoning reviewer.
Architecture, ambiguous specs, hard debugging, difficult code review, large unfamiliar repos.
Everyday Claude agent — medium features, detailed specs, first-pass review.
Mechanical edits, status/progress sync, changelogs, classification.
Tool-heavy autonomous Codex tasks, terminal/CI failures, cross-repo, prod verification.
Fallback tier only — not a default route (Luna or Sol usually win on cost/success).
OpenAI high-volume default — summaries, docs, test gen, small fixes, first-pass impl.
Feature impl, terminal-heavy, CI/build, live web/X research, cheap parallel exploration.
Default cheap executor from an explicit plan — localized UI, boilerplate, cleanup.
Same intelligence as Standard at 6x cost — only when interactive latency blocks UI iteration.

matrix.task: Classification / summaries / status sync / mechanical edits
Small, clearly-scoped edit from an explicit plan
High-risk: auth / payments / DB / security / infra

models.crossVendorReview: For high-risk changes, implementer and reviewer must be different vendors: Composer/Grok/Luna → Opus 4.8 review; Claude → GPT-5.6 Sol review; GPT-5.6 Sol → Opus 4.8 or Fable 5 review.

usage.note: Claude Code: priced from on-disk usage (PRICES verified 2026-07-10 vs model-registry.json; Opus now $5/$25, was mis-priced at Claude-3-era $15/$75). Codex: activity only (no token counts persisted). Cursor: not captured (see README). Cache-read tokens (from long sessions) are typically the dominant cost driver.
